helper to handle prefix matching in persist_device_enabled and
persist_device_released.

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge
repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt

commit.message:
Fix NRE in AddUniversalDynamicSymbol for invalid symbol address ranges (#2376)

When a ProcessSymbol event has an invalid address range (e.g.,
StartAddress=0x0, EndAddress=0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF from zeroed
/proc/kallsyms on Linux without root), the ETLX conversion crashed
with a NullReferenceException.

Three fixes applied:
1. Skip symbols where EndAddress <= StartAddress (invalid range)
2. Change (int) to (long) cast for the size parameter to match
   ForAllUnresolvedCodeAddressesInRange's parameter type
3. Add null guard on loadedModule as defense-in-depth

Fixes #2373

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: read HA user display name from Ingress headers instead of showing 'HA User'

Supervisor sends X-Remote-User-Display-Name / X-Remote-User-Name headers
on every Ingress request (HA 2024.x+). Read these to show the actual
HA user's name in the header. Falls back to MA_USERNAME / 'HA User'
for older HA versions without session user data.

commit.message:
fix: stop daemon subprocess on device disable to remove MA player

When disabling a device via POST /api/device/enabled, call
set_bt_management_enabled(False) on the active client. This stops the
daemon subprocess which disconnects from MA WebSocket, causing MA to
fire ClientRemovedEvent and unregister the player immediately.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge
repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt

commit.message:
feat: two-tier enabled/disabled — global device on/off with separate BT release

Redesign the enabled flag into a two-tier system:

1. Global enabled (config, requires restart): when enabled=false, no
   SendspinClient, BluetoothManager, port, or MA registration is created.
   Device metadata stored in state for UI display.

2. BT Release/Reclaim (hot toggle): existing functionality, only
   available when globally enabled. Releases BT adapter to host OS.

Changes:
- state.py: add disabled_devices storage (set/get with thread safety)
- sendspin_client.py: skip disabled devices entirely in main() loop
- routes/api_status.py: include disabled_devices in /api/status response
- routes/api_bt.py: add POST /api/device/enabled endpoint (restart_required)
- static/app.js: render disabled devices section, add Disable button
- templates/index.html: add #disabled-devices-section container
- static/style.css: dimmed/grayscale styling for disabled cards
- tests: 7 new tests covering state, API, and endpoint validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge

commit.message:
fix: set audio_streaming=True on stream start (not only format_change)

audio_streaming was only set True in _handle_format_change, which
doesn't fire on re-anchor or track change when the codec/format is
unchanged. After stop→start, audio_streaming stayed False even though
audio was flowing — causing red equalizer indicator despite working
audio.

Now also set audio_streaming=True on stream 'start' event when
audio_format is already configured (re-anchor, track change).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge
repository.description: A Bluetooth bridge for Music Assistant — connects your Bluetooth speakers to the MA Sendspin protocol. Runs as a Docker container, a Home Assistant addon, or a native LXC container on Proxmox VE/OpenWrt

commit.message:
fix: correct PA sink routing after audio starts

PulseAudio may ignore PULSE_SINK env var and route sink-inputs to
the default sink instead (all python3.12 processes share the same
application.name in module-stream-restore). After audio_streaming
becomes True, move our PID's sink-inputs to the correct sink.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Mycelium MVP — Federated agent orchestration on decentralized social protocols (AT Protocol, ActivityPub, Matrix)

commit.message:
docs: fix all shovel-readiness audit gaps (73→90+ target)

Fixes 15 implementation blockers, 9 ambiguities, 7 cross-doc
inconsistencies, and 8 missing-glue integration points.

ARCHITECTURE.md:
- B1: Replace broken JSON.stringify replacer with recursive canonicalize()
  function (full implementation provided)
- B2/B3: Add AgentRepository and RecordResult type definitions; repo holds
  identity for signing and optional firehose for event emission
- B4: Add Firehose container type (seq, log, subscriptions Map)
- A1: Specify filter as AND semantics
- C2: Fix phantom POSTED/DISCOVERED states in diagram → use actual status
  values (open, claimed, etc.) with explanatory note
- C3: Add overallScore to AggregatedReputation
- C4: Fix Llama 3.1 → Llama 3 typo
- C5: Add note that SCHEMAS.md is authoritative for full types
- C6: Fix taskBreakdown type to match IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN
- A3: Remove htmx/Preact → vanilla JS+CSS+SSE
- Base64url: specify Buffer.toString('base64url'), no package needed
- Mayor/Orchestrator terminology note and supervisor/labeler reserved note

IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md:
- B1: Fix signing algorithm to use canonicalize()
- B2/B3: Update putRecord signature, add upsert semantics note
- B5: Add concrete requiredCapabilities table for all 8 tasks;
  add mock claim/completion data generation strategy with TASK_ARTIFACTS map
- B8: Add step-by-step rework/rejection flow (6 steps)
- B9: Add assessment score→string mapping table (5 ranges)
- B10/B11: Define Mayor type, createMayor() signature, AgentRegistryEntry,
  in-memory registry update logic (6 firehose event handlers)
- B12: Specify agent.state lifecycle (who creates/updates and when)
- B15: Add maxConcurrentTasks column to agent roster (6 agents + mayor)
- C1: Fix task 5/6 assignment swap (forge→profile cards, atlas→firehose UI)
- C7: Add DASHBOARD_PORT and DASHBOARD_SSE_EVENT_NAME to constants
- A2: Clarify multiple claims don't require state=open
- A4: Specify putRecord as upsert
- A6: Add SSE event format spec
- A7: Clarify createStamp computes overallScore internally
- G3: Add dependency-gated posting pseudocode
- G4: Add full createStamp parameter mapping (8 params)
- G6: Clarify getStampsForAgent scans firehose.log
- G8: Add checkAndPostUnblockedTasks logic
- Add npm scripts for package.json
- Trend for 0-1 stamps: always stable
- Node.js 20 LTS requirement

INTELLIGENCE.md:
- B13: Add explicit proficiency column to capability table
  (forge: react=intermediate, api=intermediate, db=beginner)
- Add proficiency levels for all 18 agent capabilities

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  capabilities/domains/benchmarks, agent-to-model assignment table,

  bootstrap function shapes and record construction examples

- Module dependency graph: 7-layer import hierarchy showing exact
  build order and what each src/ file imports

- Mayor = Orchestrator terminology clarification
- supervisor/labeler agentTypes reserved-for-future note

IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md additions:
- Phase 0: Node.js 20 LTS requirement, pinned dependency versions
- Bootstrap Sequence: exact 10-step initialization order (Firehose first,
  Mayor subscription second, then intelligence, then agents)
- maxConcurrentTasks: documented as advisory/soft limit (no enforcement)
- Simulated Time Model: table explaining real delay vs. executionTime
  field values; per-task pre-defined executionTime for all 8 demo tasks
- Constants section: all magic numbers in one src/constants.ts reference
  (execution times, reputation weights, trust thresholds, trend detection,
  ranking weights, firehose seq start)
- Reconcile base times: canonical values are low=2s/medium=5s/high=10s

SCHEMAS.md additions:
- Capability tag normalization rules (kebab-case, exact match, INTELLIGENCE.md ref)
- Intelligence Model cross-reference to INTELLIGENCE.md for concrete values

E2E-SCENARIO.md additions:

commit.message:
fix: prevent false zombie restarts when audio is actually playing

Zombie detection now only triggers when audio has NEVER arrived in
the current subprocess session. Brief streaming gaps (re-anchor,
group sync, track changes) no longer cause false positives — PA
buffers keep playing during these gaps so restarting is wrong.

Reverted timeout back to 15s since it now only applies to genuinely
stuck subprocesses that never received audio data.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: mute PA sinks directly on shutdown instead of pausing players

Replace subprocess pause command with direct PA sink muting in
_graceful_shutdown(). This ensures audio cuts cleanly regardless of
how the restart is triggered (systemd, Docker, HA auto-update, CLI)
instead of relying on the UI to pre-mute before restart.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: switch intelligence providers to GitHub Models (cloud) + Ollama (local)

Replace 3-provider setup (Anthropic, OpenAI, Ollama) with a 2-provider
architecture for prototyping:
- GitHub Models: unified cloud gateway for Claude, GPT, Phi, and other
  commercial models available via GitHub's public catalog
- Local Ollama: self-hosted inference for open-source models (Llama 3 70B, CodeLlama)

Changes across all PRD docs:
- ARCHITECTURE.md: updated IntelligenceProvider/Model types (added endpoint,
  modelOrigin fields); added MVP Provider Strategy section; updated bootstrap flow
- E2E-SCENARIO.md: updated provider tree, model counts, all intelligence
  attribution strings, character sheets, and dashboard widget labels

repository.name: sendspin-bt-bridge

commit.message:
fix: startup unmute watcher killing daemon after 60s timeout

Two bugs in _startup_unmute_watcher introduced in smooth restart:
1. unmute_task was in asyncio.wait(FIRST_COMPLETED) — its completion
   after 60s timeout killed the daemon. Now fire-and-forget.
2. Unconditional unmute on timeout even when no audio streamed —
   now skips unmute if audio_streaming was never True.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: remove legacy move-sink-input routing from bridge_daemon

PULSE_SINK env var in each subprocess handles routing from first sample.

leftover that could itself cause PA glitches triggering re-anchoring.

import, os import. amove_pid_sink_inputs remains in pulse.py for diagnostics.

repository.name: Portal-Upravnik
repository.description: Public

commit.message:
feat: smooth restart — mute sinks, cache sink names, auto-unmute

Three improvements for glitch-free service restarts:

1. Startup sink mute: daemon_process mutes PA sink before BridgeDaemon
   starts, _startup_unmute_watcher unmutes after audio_streaming=True
   + 1.5s stabilization delay (60s timeout safety)

2. Pre-restart local mute: saveAndRestart() mutes all local PA sinks
   via force_local before stopping — no MA pause/play needed, so sync
   group members on other bridges keep playing uninterrupted

3. Sink name cache: configure_bluetooth_audio() saves found sink name
   to LAST_SINKS[mac] in config.json; on restart tries cached name
   first, skipping 3s A2DP delay + retries if still valid

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: rewrite deploy workflow using official fastapi cloud pattern

The CLI reads FASTAPI_CLOUD_TOKEN and FASTAPI_CLOUD_APP_ID from env
vars directly (via Identity._get_token_from_env). No auth.json writing,
no login step, no cloud.json needed.

Uses astral-sh/setup-uv@v7 and actions/checkout@v5 (also fixes the
Node.js 20 deprecation warnings).

matching how the CLI stores credentials locally.

repository.name: hood-ball
repository.description: Accelerate and smash your way to victory in a car-based multiplayer soccer battle

commit.message:
fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas (#2)

* fix: allow optional percentage in summarize schemas

SummarizeRequest and SummarizeAgainRequest had `percentage: int` with
`ge=30, le=80`, which rejected `0` or `null` from the frontend with a
422 Unprocessable Entity.

Changed both to `int | None` with `ge=1, le=100` so callers can omit
or null-out the field; `_get_percentage()` already handles the
default fallback to a random 30-80 value.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: widen percentage range to 1-100 across models and simplify fallback

- Text model: percentage ge=30,le=80 → ge=1,le=100
- Summary model: same range update
- _get_percentage: replace random 30-80 fallback with deterministic
  default of 50%; respect user's explicit choice via schema validation
- Remove unused `random` import

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: add GitHub Actions workflow to auto-deploy to FastAPI Cloud

Triggers on every push to main. Installs fastapi-cli, restores the
auth token from the FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH GitHub secret, then runs
`fastapi deploy . --no-wait` which uses the app_id from .fastapicloud/cloud.json.

Setup required:
1. Copy the contents of ~/.fastapi/auth.json on your local machine
2. Add it as a GitHub secret named FASTAPI_CLOUD_AUTH in the repo settings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: fix deploy workflow to use uv + PAT token auth

- Use uv sync to install deps (matches project toolchain)
- Authenticate with `fastapi cloud login --token` using PAT
- Remove incorrect auth.json approach
- Run fastapi via `uv run` to use project venv

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* ci: recreate .fastapicloud/cloud.json in CI before deploy

Since .fastapicloud is gitignored, the folder won't exist after
checkout. Recreate cloud.json from GitHub secrets so `fastapi deploy .`
can find the app_id and team_id.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: code review hardening — security fixes, narrowed exceptions, 30 new tests

Security:
- Fix MFA session variable leak (_ha_login_user cleared at all auth paths)
- Add MAC address validation (strict regex before bluetoothctl calls)
- Remove silent first-device fallback (proper 400 errors)

Improved:
- Split 260-line login() into 4 per-flow handlers
- Extract shared get_client_or_error() and validate_mac() helpers
- Narrow 27 broad except Exception to specific types across 6 modules
- Atomic config writes with tempfile + rename
- Add 30s BT scan cooldown (HTTP 429)
- Replace 7 magic numbers with named constants
- Deduplicate IP detection via get_local_ip()
- Move imports to module level, add type annotations

Tests:
- test_client_lookup.py: 17 tests (multi-device lookup, MAC injection)
- test_mfa_session.py: 9 tests (session lifecycle, cross-user leak)
- test_scan_cooldown.py: 4 tests (429 cooldown, 409 concurrent)

Total: 180 tests passing (was 150)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: resolve PR #16 review comments

- Guard rand.Int63n against panic when delay < 2ns (client.go)
- Prevent internal error leaking in ToHTTPError fallback (apperror.go)
- Standardize API error response format with consistent code field (responses.go)
- Use errors.As in panic recovery middleware (middleware.go)
- Fix ErrCircuitBreakerOpen docstring and transitionTo comment (circuitbreaker.go)
- Fix halfOpenCalls not reset on OPEN->HALF_OPEN transition (circuitbreaker.go)
- Deduplicate stuck backup alerts with StuckAlertedAt timestamp (scheduler.go)
- Guard SetAlerter with runningMutex on scheduler (scheduler.go)
- Guard SetAlerter with backupMutex on orchestrator (orchestrator.go)
- Replace time.Sleep with channel sync in circuit breaker tests
- Replace time.Sleep with channel/WaitGroup sync in alert tests
- Update error handling docs to reflect all changes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: UX improvements — replace alerts with toasts, mobile scroll, rate-limit handling, session lifetime

- Replace all alert() calls with showToast() for non-blocking feedback
- Surface backend validation errors in save toast messages
- Add save button disable during config POST to prevent double-clicks
- Improve mobile BT table scroll with fade hint and smaller min-widths
- Add GitHub API rate-limit header handling in update checker
- Handle HTTP 403 rate-limit responses gracefully with warning log
- Set explicit 24h session lifetime with PERMANENT_SESSION_LIFETIME

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: hide Sign out in HA addon mode, show username only

In HA addon mode the logout action is meaningless — Ingress
re-authenticates automatically. Show HA username without sign-out link.
Standalone mode keeps Sign out as before.

commit.message:
fix: overlay/sidebar z-index stacking — overlay was blocking sidebar navigation (#520)

* Initial plan

* fix: correct overlay/sidebar z-index stacking to prevent click blocking

- Change --z-overlay from 1000 to 40 in :root (now below --z-sidebar: 200)
- Update :root scale comment to show correct order: base(1) < overlay(40) < dropdown(100) < sidebar(200) < modal(2000) < toast(3000)
- Migrate .sidebar from hardcoded z-index:70 to z-index:var(--z-sidebar) (200)
- Migrate .hamburger from hardcoded z-index:80 to z-index:calc(var(--z-sidebar)+1) (201)
- Migrate .topRightBar from hardcoded z-index:95 to z-index:calc(var(--z-sidebar)+5) (205)
- Add required comment above .overlay: "Overlay must remain below sidebar..."
- Update docs/z-index-inventory.md to reflect completed migrations

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
docs: add module-level CHANGELOG, SECURITY, and AUDIT for ingestion, llm, and metadata

- src/ingestion/: v1.5.0 production ingestion layer; 14 connectors, LLM
  deontic pipeline, SSRF/path-traversal/prompt-injection mitigations, 19
  test targets, 0 open security issues.
- src/llm/: v1.16.0 full-featured LLM module; 151 source files, VRAM
  isolation, LoRA SHA-256 integrity, prompt injection detection, 28 test
  targets; open: federated inference (#1928), best-effort cancellation,
  synthetic speculative-decoding logits.
- src/metadata/: v1.5.x metadata layer; 12 source files, INFORMATION_SCHEMA
  RBAC, AST-based migration scripts, schema audit log, 14+ test targets;
  security audit planned v1.6.0.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: require password before enabling authentication

Server-side: POST /api/config rejects AUTH_ENABLED=true when no
AUTH_PASSWORD_HASH exists (skipped in HA addon mode).
Client-side: saveConfig() blocks and focuses password field.
GET /api/config now returns _password_set boolean flag.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  queries, scheduled edge refresh, EXPLAIN endpoint; open issues #1829
  #1830 #1832 documented
- importers: 31-file module covering SQL/NoSQL/S3/BigQuery/Kafka/MDM/
  CRDT/GUI wizard; all security mitigations and open GraphQL depth-limit
  and federated-learning review items documented
- index: HNSW/GPU/PQ/BQ/RQ/DiskANN/ScaNN/R-tree/B-tree/full-text/
  multi-GPU; open issues #1872 #1878 #1882-#1887 documented; tenant
  key prefix format tenant:<id>:<index_name> and VRAM secure-clear
  controls described

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: add CHANGELOG.md, SECURITY.md, AUDIT.md for 16 source modules

Creates module-specific documentation for all 16 ThemisDB source modules
(acceleration, analytics, api, aql, auth, base, cache, cdc, chimera,
config, content, core, exporters, geo, governance, gpu).

Each module receives three files:
- CHANGELOG.md: Keep a Changelog format documenting version history
  derived from each module's ROADMAP.md completed items
- SECURITY.md: Module-specific threat model, security controls, data
  handling, known limitations, and dependency security
- AUDIT.md: Code audit report with source file inventory, test coverage
  status, build system verification, open/resolved findings, and
  compliance notes

All content is specific to each module based on its ROADMAP.md and
ARCHITECTURE.md — no generic placeholder content.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
